content.problemStatement:
You are given MMM triplets (X1,Y1,B1),(X2,Y2,B2),…,(XM,YM,BM)(X_1, Y_1, B_1), (X_2, Y_2, B_2), \ldots, (X_M, Y_M, B_M)(X1​,Y1​,B1​),(X2​,Y2​,B2​),…,(XM​,YM​,BM​). Find the number of sequences of positive integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ such that for each valid iii, lcm(AXi,AYi)=Bi\mathrm{lcm}(A_{X_i},A_{Y_i}) = B_ilcm(AXi​​,AYi​​)=Bi​, or determine that there is an infinite number of such sequences.

Since the answer can be very large, compute it modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7). Note that the values B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​ can also be very large ― you are given their prime decompositions.
constraints: 1≤Xi,Yi≤N1 \le X_i, Y_i \le N1≤Xi​,Yi​≤N for each valid iii
prp_rpr​ is a prime for each valid rrr
the total number of different primes prp_rpr​ in each test case does not exceed 555
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (20 points, time limit 2 seconds):** N≤20N \le 20N≤20
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
MMM lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M), the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers XiX_iXi​, YiY_iYi​ and RiR_iRi​, followed by a space and 2Ri2R_i2Ri​ space-separated integers p1,e1,p2,e2,…,pRi,eRip_1, e_1, p_2, e_2, \ldots, p_{R_i}, e_{R_i}p1​,e1​,p2​,e2​,…,pRi​​,eRi​​, denoting that Bi=∏r=1RiprerB_i = \prod_{r=1}^{R_i} p_r^{e_r}Bi​=∏r=1Ri​​prer​​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of valid sequences modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007, or −1-1−1 if there are infinitely many valid sequences.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Both elements of AAA can be any positive integers, so there is an infinite number of valid sequences.
Example case 2: There are two constraints, but they are actually identical: lcm(A1,A2)=1\mathrm{lcm}(A_1, A_2) = 1lcm(A1​,A2​)=1. The only sequence that satisfies this is A=[1,1]A = [1, 1]A=[1,1].
Example case 3: The two constraints contradict each other, so there is no valid sequence.
